steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question tests subject-verb agreement. The blank is the verb inside the relative clause "that ______ in contact with it," which modifies "nematodes." The relative pronoun "that" refers to "nematodes," making the plural noun the subject of the blank verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — has come: singular present-perfect verb; violates subject-verb agreement with plural subject "nematodes." ✗
  • Choice B — comes: singular simple-present verb; violates subject-verb agreement with plural subject "nematodes." ✗
  • Choice C — is coming: singular present-progressive verb; violates subject-verb agreement with plural subject "nematodes." ✗
  • Choice D — come: plural simple-present verb; correctly agrees in number with plural subject "nematodes." ✓
That confirms D is correct.
